pattern: Since every split must immediately produce two good pieces, the whole array collapses to singletons only if some neighboring pair can already stand alone.
hints: Work out what the very first cut has to look like: both halves it creates must already be good on their own.
A piece only ever needs to survive its first split as two good halves; sums never grow as you keep cutting, so look for a single strong neighboring pair rather than simulating every cut.
The array is splittable exactly when its length is at most 2, or some two adjacent elements already sum to at least m.

edge_inputs.description: an adjacent pair reaches the threshold
no adjacent pair reaches the threshold
threshold met further along the array, not at the first pair
two-element array is trivially splittable regardless of sum
the qualifying pair sits at the very end of the array
every element alone is below m but no neighbor pair clears it either
sum of the whole array exceeds m but no two neighbors do

stress.description: 100 small values with a single qualifying neighbor pair planted near the middle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    m = 150
    nums = [rng.randrange(1, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    if n >= 2:
        i = n // 2
        nums[i] = 90
        nums[i + 1] = 90
    return [nums, m]
